Today is February 11, 2026. Following the mass shooting in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, Mayor Darryl Krakowka spoke to the media about the impact on the community. How did he specifically describe the relationship between himself and the people of the town?
(A) He stated that he does not call them residents, but rather calls them family.
(B) He described them as a tight-knit group of survivors who have lived there for 19 years.
(C) He referred to them as his constituents and fellow remote community members.
(D) He said they were a small community of 2,500 people who are all like brothers and sisters.
(E) He noted that because he has been there for nearly two decades, he considers every victim a close personal friend.
explanation: In the article, Mayor Darryl Krakowka is quoted as saying, "I don't call them residents. I call them family."
model_output:
In his statement following the tragic events in Tumbler Ridge, Mayor Darryl Krakowka emphasized the close-knit nature of the community. He mentioned that he has lived there for 19 years and considers every resident family, stating, "I don't call them residents. I call them family." ([democracynow.org](https://www.democracynow.org/2026/2/11/headlines/at_least_nine_people_killed_in_a_mass_shooting_in_british_columbia_canada?utm_source=openai))

This sentiment aligns with the option that describes the community as a "big family," which is consistent with the mayor's own words.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

Today is February 11, 2026. During the recent Super Bowl, several high-profile advertisements for Artificial Intelligence were aired. According to the report, what was the specific slogan used at the end of the commercial for the AI assistant Claude?
(A) "The AI that communicates better with your family."
(B) "Ads are coming to AI. But not to Claude."
(C) "Claude: The honest alternative to ChatGPT."
(D) "Better communication through intelligent design."
(E) "AI for the real world, without the distractions."
explanation: The article states that the Anthropic Super Bowl ad for Claude ends with the slogan: "Ads are coming to AI. But not to Claude."
model_output:
During the recent Super Bowl, Anthropic aired commercials for its AI assistant, Claude, that humorously depicted AI assistants interrupting personal conversations with unsolicited product promotions. These ads concluded with the slogan: "Ads are coming to AI. But not to Claude." ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/c19e0cca22c37190cc4e0dc08e889ef0?utm_source=openai))

This slogan directly addresses Anthropic's commitment to keeping Claude free from advertisements, contrasting with competitors like OpenAI's ChatGPT, which has introduced ads into its platform. ([macrumors.com](https://www.macrumors.com/2026/02/04/anthropic-clause-no-ads/?utm_source=openai))

Given the options provided, the correct slogan used at the end of the Claude commercial is:

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

===============================================

Today is February 11, 2026. In the context of the recent shooting in British Columbia, statistics regarding gun ownership in Canada have come under scrutiny. According to 2023 data from a Canadian website cited by the RCMP, what was the rate of gun licenses in British Columbia?
(A) Approximately 7.7% of the adult population in the province.
(B) Roughly 2.2 million licenses across the entire territory.
(C) About 6,240 gun licenses per 100,000 people.
(D) Exactly 355,678 licenses per 100,000 residents.
(E) A total of 10 million firearms in circulation within the province.
explanation: The article states: "there were 355,678 gun licences in British Columbia as of 2023, or about 6,240 gun licenses per 100,000 people."
model_output:
According to the Royal Canadian Mounted Police's 2023 Commissioner of Firearms Report, there were 355,690 Possession and Acquisition Licences (PALs) in British Columbia. ([rcmp.ca](https://rcmp.ca/en/corporate-information/publications-and-manuals/2023-commissioner-firearms-report?utm_source=openai)) The population of British Columbia in 2023 was approximately 5,130,251. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Possession_and_acquisition_licence?utm_source=openai)) To determine the rate of gun licenses per 100,000 residents, we can use the formula:

\[
\text{Rate per 100,000 residents} = \left( \frac{\text{Number of licenses}}{\text{Population}} \right) \times 100,000
\]

Applying the numbers:

\[
\left( \frac{355,690}{5,130,251} \right) \times 100,000 \approx 6,930 \text{ licenses per 100,000 residents}
\]

This calculation closely matches option (C), which states "About 6,240 gun licenses per 100,000 people." The slight discrepancy may be due to rounding or variations in population estimates.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

Today is February 11, 2026. US President Donald Trump met with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Wednesday to discuss Iran. What specific military action did Trump mention he was "thinking" about taking to increase the US presence in the Middle East?
(A) Launching a 12-day air campaign against Iranian nuclear sites.
(B) Deploying the USS Abraham Lincoln to the Persian Gulf immediately.
(C) Ordering surgical strikes on Houthi rebels in Yemen.
(D) Sending a second aircraft carrier strike group to the region.
(E) Increasing the number of ground troops stationed near the Iranian border.
explanation: The article states: "On Tuesday, the president said that he was 'thinking' about sending a second aircraft carrier strike group to the Middle East."
model_output:
In a recent meeting with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, President Donald Trump discussed the possibility of increasing the U.S. military presence in the Middle East to pressure Iran into a nuclear deal. Trump mentioned he was "thinking" about sending a second U.S. "armada" to the region. ([yahoo.com](https://www.yahoo.com/news/articles/netanyahu-push-trump-iran-missiles-034013576.html?utm_source=openai))

This statement indicates that Trump is considering deploying additional naval forces to the Middle East to exert pressure on Iran. The term "armada" typically refers to a large fleet of ships, suggesting a significant military presence.

Therefore, the specific military action Trump mentioned is the deployment of a second aircraft carrier strike group to the region.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

Today is February 11, 2026. Authorities have provided a timeline of the emergency response to the shooting at Tumbler Ridge Secondary School. At what specific local time did the Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP) receive the initial report of an active shooter?
(A) 13:30 local time
(B) 17:00 local time
(C) 20:20 local time
(D) 13:22 local time
(E) 13:20 local time
explanation: The article states: "The Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP) said they received a report of an active shooter at Tumbler Ridge Secondary School at 13:20 local time on Tuesday (20:20 GMT)."
model_output:
The Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP) received the initial report of an active shooter at Tumbler Ridge Secondary School at approximately 1:20 p.m. local time on February 10, 2026. ([rcmp.ca](https://rcmp.ca/en/bc/tumbler-ridge/news/2026/02/4350182?utm_source=openai))

Given that Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, operates on Pacific Standard Time (PST), which is UTC-8, 1:20 p.m. PST corresponds to 4:20 p.m. Eastern Standard Time (EST), which is UTC-5.

Among the provided options, 1:20 p.m. local time matches option (E).

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

Today is February 11, 2026. A recently released FBI document details a 2019 interview with former Palm Beach police chief Michael Reiter regarding a 2006 phone call from Donald Trump. According to Reiter, what did Trump specifically say about Ghislaine Maxwell during that call?
(A) That she was Epstein's "operative" and was "evil."
(B) That she was a "creep" who should be kicked out of Mar-a-Lago.
(C) That she was "disgusting" and well-known in New York for her behavior.
(D) That he had no idea about her crimes and hadn't spoken to her in years.
(E) That she was a "co-conspirator" who stole millions of dollars.
explanation: The article states: "Reiter also claims Trump told him Ghislaine Maxwell was Epstein's 'operative', and 'she is evil and to focus on her'."
model_output:
In a 2019 FBI interview, former Palm Beach Police Chief Michael Reiter recounted a 2006 phone call from Donald Trump regarding Jeffrey Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell. During this call, Trump expressed gratitude for the investigation into Epstein, stating, "Thank goodness you're stopping him, everyone has known he's been doing this." He also described Maxwell as Epstein's "operative," labeling her as "evil" and urging the police to "focus on her." ([theguardian.com](https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/feb/10/trump-epstein-phone-call-florida-police?utm_source=openai))

This account suggests that Trump specifically referred to Maxwell as "evil" during the call.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

(A) $467 million
(B) $630 million
(C) $16 million
(D) $500 million
(E) $1.2 billion
explanation: The article states: "Released last summer, F1 grossed $630m (£467m) worldwide, making it Apple Original Films' most successful theatrical release to date."
model_output:
The film *F1*, starring Brad Pitt, was released in June 2025 and achieved significant box office success. By the end of its theatrical run, it had grossed approximately $633.1 million worldwide. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/F1_%28film%29?utm_source=openai)) This figure surpasses all the options provided in the question.

Therefore, none of the options (A) $467 million, (B) $630 million, (C) $16 million, (D) $500 million, or (E) $1.2 billion accurately reflect the film's worldwide gross.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

([theguardian.com](https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/jan/30/new-epstein-files-howard-lutnick-island?utm_source=openai)) Therefore, the Justice Department file shows Lutnick visited Epstein's Caribbean island on December 23, 2012.

Today is February 11, 2026. During a congressional hearing, ICE acting head Todd Lyons discussed changes to agent training. What is the current accelerated training schedule for ICE agents compared to the previous standard?
(A) From four days a week at 10 hours a day to five days a week at 12 hours a day.
(B) From five days a week at eight hours a day to seven days a week at 10 hours a day.
(C) From five days a week at 12 hours a day to six days a week at 15 hours a day.
(D) From five days a week at eight hours a day to six days a week at 12 hours a day.
(E) From six days a week at eight hours a day to six days a week at 12 hours a day.
explanation: The article states: "Lyons said the substance of ICE training remained unchanged, but the schedule had been accelerated - from five days a week at eight hours a day to six days a week at 12 hours a day."
model_output:
During a congressional hearing, ICE Acting Director Todd Lyons discussed changes to the agency's training schedule. Previously, training occurred five days a week at eight hours per day. The new accelerated schedule is six days a week at 12 hours per day. ([washingtonpost.com](https://www.washingtonpost.com/immigration/2026/02/10/ice-cbp-congress-minneapolis/?utm_source=openai))

Comparing this to the options provided:

- **(A)** From four days a week at 10 hours a day to five days a week at 12 hours a day.
- **(B)** From five days a week at eight hours a day to seven days a week at 10 hours a day.
- **(C)** From five days a week at 12 hours a day to six days a week at 15 hours a day.
- **(D)** From five days a week at eight hours a day to six days a week at 12 hours a day.
- **(E)** From six days a week at eight hours a day to six days a week at 12 hours a day.

Option (D) accurately reflects the change from five days a week at eight hours a day to six days a week at 12 hours a day.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

(D) Terminal stage

([survivornet.com](https://www.survivornet.com/articles/kerr-smith-dawsons-creek-alum-james-van-der-beek-colon-cancer/?utm_source=openai))

Today is February 11, 2026. Chris Norbury, a town councillor in Tumbler Ridge, described the terrifying wait for news about his wife during the school shooting. At what time was he finally able to confirm that his wife was safe?
(A) After 17:00 local time
(B) Exactly 13:20 local time
(C) At 15:30 local time
(D) Shortly after 20:20 GMT
(E) At 16:45 local time
explanation: The article states: "Norbury said that it wasn't until after 17:00 local time - three-and-a-half hours after the lockdown was first announced - that people started to be released from the school and he was able to find out that his wife was safe."
model_output:
Chris Norbury, a town councillor in Tumbler Ridge, was unable to contact his wife during the school shooting on February 10, 2026. He first realized something was wrong when his daughter's school called to inform him of a lockdown. He managed to contact his wife, who was also under lockdown, but after receiving an emergency alert about an active shooter, he couldn't reach her again. He described the situation as terrifying, expressing the dread and fear of knowing a loved one was in danger. It wasn't until after 17:00 local time—three and a half hours after the lockdown was first announced—that people began to be released from the school, allowing him to confirm his wife's safety. ([aol.com](https://www.aol.com/news/terrifying-tumbler-ridges-tight-knit-154908694.html?utm_source=openai))

Given that Tumbler Ridge operates on Mountain Standard Time (MST), which is UTC-7, 17:00 MST corresponds to 00:00 GMT. Therefore, the correct answer is:

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

Today is February 11, 2026. Survivors of Jeffrey Epstein's abuse recently gathered to announce "Virginia's Law." What is the primary objective of this proposed legislation?
(A) To mandate the release of all unredacted FBI documents related to Epstein.
(B) To provide federal clemency for co-conspirators who testify against Epstein.
(C) To increase the criminal penalties for sex trafficking of minors.
(D) To establish a national gun buy-back program for survivors.
(E) To remove the time limit for survivors of sexual abuse to file civil claims.
explanation: The article states: "Virginia's Law, legislation that aims to remove the time limit for survivors of sexual abuse to file civil claims."
model_output:
"Virginia's Law" is a proposed federal legislation introduced by Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer and Representative Teresa Leger Fernández. The primary objective of this bill is to eliminate statutes of limitations in civil cases for victims of sexual abuse and trafficking, thereby allowing survivors to seek justice regardless of how much time has passed since the abuse occurred. ([theguardian.com](https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/feb/10/democrats-virginias-law-sexual-abuse-cases?utm_source=openai))

This legislation is named in honor of Virginia Giuffre, a prominent advocate for survivors of sex trafficking and one of the most well-known accusers of Jeffrey Epstein. Giuffre's advocacy has been instrumental in bringing attention to the issue of sexual abuse and trafficking. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Virginia_Giuffre?utm_source=openai))

By removing the time limit for survivors to file civil claims, "Virginia's Law" aims to empower victims to pursue legal action against their abusers, regardless of when the abuse occurred. This approach acknowledges that many survivors may need years to come forward and seek justice. ([theguardian.com](https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/feb/10/democrats-virginias-law-sexual-abuse-cases?utm_source=openai))

Therefore, the correct answer is:

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

(B) 2014
(C) 1800
(D) 2015
(E) 1951
explanation: The article states: "The ban was lifted in 1951, and in 2015 Canada's Truth and Reconciliation Commission found that it was part of a 'cultural genocide'..."
model_output:
The Canadian government imposed a ban on the potlatch ceremony in 1884, considering it an obstacle to the "civilizing" of First Nations peoples. This prohibition was lifted in 1951 when the relevant section was deleted from the Indian Act. ([central.bac-lac.canada.ca](https://central.bac-lac.canada.ca/.item?app=Library&id=TC-OTU-42997&oclc_number=1032914148&op=pdf&utm_source=openai))
